codeReview.summary: The architecture section is substantial and mostly polished, but it misses one explicit feature requirement and introduces a cross-surface maturity mismatch that overstates readiness.

issues.description: The feature description calls for stable-ID-backed subsystem dependencies, and `CostBandPhase.dependencies` is documented as phase IDs, but the cost-band models store human-readable phase labels such as `"Phase 1: Foundation Models"` instead. Because `CostBandExplorer` renders those strings verbatim, the architecture cost explorer is not actually backed by stable dependency identifiers and will break referential integrity if labels change.

The flywheel marks `Strategy Agents & Vaults` as `in_progress`, but the rest of the site still presents strategy agents as planned (`src/content/narrative.ts:198`, `src/content/roadmap-nodes.ts:98`, `src/components/tokenomics/TokenUtilitySurface.tsx:29`). Collapsing vaults and agents into a single in-progress node makes the architecture surface overstate agent readiness and violates the repo's cross-section status-consistency requirement.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The shared content-model guidance does not spell out how cost-band dependency references should be modeled or validated, even though the type contract expects ID-based references. That gap likely contributed to string-label dependencies being shipped in the architecture cost explorer.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/library/content-model.md` only documents readable dependency notes for narrative confidence frames, while `src/content/types.ts:289` says `CostBandPhase.dependencies` are phase IDs. `src/content/__tests__/content-models.test.ts:171-176` validates roadmap dependency IDs, but there is no analogous assertion for cost-band dependencies.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The frontend worker skill requires browser validation but does not document practical `agent-browser` fallbacks for precise scroll/interaction, which caused trial-and-error during verification.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: The transcript skeleton shows failed `agent-browser click "Architecture"`, `execute`, and `js` attempts before the worker discovered `agent-browser eval` as the usable approach. `src/.factory/skills/frontend-experience-worker/SKILL.md` instructs workers to use `agent-browser`, but does not capture this interaction pattern.

summary: Fail: the architecture atlas ships a user-visible status inconsistency for strategy agents and does not implement the promised stable-ID-backed cost-band dependencies.
